During the Cold War, the United States and the Soviet Union were the two primary superpowers in conflict. In the United States, several Presidents held office and managed foreign policy during this tense period. Dwight D. Eisenhower was the first to lead during the early years, followed by John F. Kennedy, Lyndon B. Johnson, Richard Nixon, Gerald Ford, Jimmy Carter, Ronald Reagan, and George H. W. Bush. In the Soviet Union, leaders such as Joseph Stalin, Nikita Khrushchev, Leonid Brezhnev, and Mikhail Gorbachev held power at different times. These individuals shaped the policies and tensions that defined the Cold War era.
